name: Density Matrix Toolkit
summary: A way of representing mixed states, partial knowledge, and open-system noise when pure-state vectors are no longer enough.
whenToUseIt: Use it when systems interact with the environment, when only part of a larger state is observed, or when error models matter.
steps: Represent the state as a density matrix instead of a single ket.
Use trace and positivity checks to confirm a valid state description.
Apply channels and measurement rules in matrix form.
Take partial traces to study subsystems.
Extract observables such as purity, entropy, or fidelity.
example: A qubit affected by dephasing is better described with a density matrix because coherence decays even when the state probabilities stay unchanged.
